tasks.description: Stelle sicher, dass das Phone-Modul die Wearable Play Services dependency hat.

tests.expect: Build completes without unresolved symbol errors for com.google.android.gms.wearable

tasks.description: Erzeuge eine Hilfsklasse, die Settings als persistenten DataItem an die Watch pusht.
tasks.apply_to: phone module (package matching phone app, e.g. de.recklessgreed.football.refereegamedaycompanion)

create_files.content_template: class PhoneWearSettingsSender { public static void sendSettingsToWear(Context context, int timeoutsPerHalf, String homeTeamName, String awayTeamName) { /* PutDataMapRequest to '/settings' with keys: timeoutsPerHalf:int, homeTeamName:string, awayTeamName:string, timestamp:long */ } }

static_methods.signature: public static void sendSettingsToWear(Context context, int timeoutsPerHalf, @Nullable String homeTeamName, @Nullable String awayTeamName)
static_methods.behavior: Packt keys in PutDataMapRequest('/settings'), setzt 'timestamp', ruft Wearable.getDataClient(context).putDataItem(request), loggt success/failure

api_contract.notes: Setze PutDataRequest.setUrgent() um schnellere Übertragung zu forcieren. Verwende konsistente Log-Tag 'SettingsSyncPhone'.

steps: Auf Phone: rufe sendSettingsToWear(...) direkt nach Settings-Änderung auf.
Auf Watch: überwache Logcat (Tag 'WearSettingsSync') und prüfe, dass applySettings ausgeführt wurde.

tasks.description: Rufe PhoneWearSettingsSender.sendSettingsToWear(...) immer dann auf, wenn Settings in der Phone-App geschrieben/updated werden.
tasks.apply_to: Phone app: SettingsProvider.insert/update or SettingsActivity where settings are changed

actions.location_hint: Am Ende von SettingsProvider.insert(...) und SettingsProvider.update(...), nach erfolgreichem DB-write

steps: Prüfe Logcat: Phone loggt 'Settings pushed to wear' und Watch loggt 'Applying settings'.

tasks.description: Optional: implementiere MessageClient listener, damit die Uhr aktiv Settings anfordern kann.

api_contract.behavior_on_message: read current settings from local provider and call PhoneWearSettingsSender.sendSettingsToWear(context, ...)

create_files.content_template: class PhoneWearMessageListener implements MessageClient.OnMessageReceivedListener { public void onMessageReceived(MessageEvent event) { if ("/request_settings".equals(event.getPath())) { // read settings from provider then call PhoneWearSettingsSender.sendSettingsToWear(...) } } }

steps: Auf Watch: sende Message via MessageClient to '/request_settings'.
Auf Phone: prüfe, dass Phone empfängt und 'Settings pushed to wear' loggt.

tasks.description: Verwende konsistente Log-Tags, damit Tests/Agenten Logs leicht erkennen.

tests.expect: Phone logs 'Settings pushed to wear', Watch logs 'Applying settings' and 'Updated local provider'
